attacking_style: Not an attacker in traditional sense; launches sprints from 200-300m out when positioned correctly, occasionally joins breakaways (249km stage) purely for green jersey intermediate points, showing tactical opportunism beyond pure speed.
team_tactics: Heavily reliant on elite leadout execution (Mørkøv critical), Quick-Step/Astana teams commit full resources to positioning through final 3km. When leadout fails or mechanical occurs inside 1km, results collapse—no Plan B improvisation ability remains.
terrain_preference: Pure pan-flat bunch sprints; explicitly vulnerable on any categorized climb (dropped consistently on 4% gradients). Self-identifies crosswind stages as easiest for positioning. Technical wet finishes expose handling limitations (white line slips recorded multiple times).
vulnerability: Catastrophic decline in bunch positioning ability through technical finales and narrow infrastructure—trapped behind weak pilots, caught in crashes despite course experience. Any gradient over 3% eliminates him. Late-race mechanicals (3 recorded inside final 3km) suggest equipment handling degradation.
key_patterns: Dropped on first categorized climb consistently, even controlled 4% gradients (4 observations)
White line/wet surface handling failures in sprint finales, technical skill decline evident (3 observations)
Trapped in poor positioning behind inferior pilots in final 2km when leadout fails (5+ observations)
Still wins from distance (300m) when positioned correctly, raw power intact for age (4 wins documented)
Crashes in technical finales despite prior course knowledge, positioning deterioration confirmed (4+ crash observations)
Requires elite leadout pilot (Mørkøv/Renshaw); no ability to self-position in modern compressed sprints (8+ observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tour showed significant positioning decline versus 2021-2022 wins—trapped outside top-20 regularly, age diminishing technical navigation despite retained raw sprint power when delivered correctly
